human_verification.notes: Reviewed scheduling and dispatch boundaries, home/field service artifacts, healthcare prerequisite handling, authorization/referral/eligibility dependencies, PHI controls, clinical and payer escalation rules, source authority mapping, scenario coverage, and functionality coverage.

review_notes: All required domain research scenario families are mapped to explicit scenario IDs.
All shared, planning-scheduling-and-allocation, and healthcare-and-care-delivery abilities are validated and covered.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for this validated specification-based package.
No unreviewed regressions are present in this package build.

specialist_owned_evidence: Authority boundaries are explicit for clinical judgment, visit frequency, plan-of-care alteration, payer interpretation, authorization approval, PHI disclosure, coding, claim disposition, final dispatch, route approval, staffing approval, overbook approval, and field-safety release.

Research sources cover public healthcare regulator guidance, public payer/provider operating guidance, recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance, and home-services consumer or field-service guidance.

Scenario map covers normal home health scheduling, missing prerequisites, capacity shortfall, priority conflict, final approval refusal, PHI handling, clinical escalation, adjacent revenue-cycle rejection, low-confidence routing, policy conflict, tenant terminology, and field disruption refresh.
Commercialization and deployment artifacts define specification-based operation and separate retained-implementation promotion criteria.
notes: The pack is safe for specification-based runtime and does not claim trusted built authority.
Promotion to retained implementation is deferred to a separate explicit build, evidence, and approval event.
